intro: Hieracium stenolonchum, a member of the dandelion-like genus Hieracium, is a perennial plant native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues, due to its purported anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to soothe and protect the gastrointestinal tract. Bioactive constituents present in Hieracium stenolonchum include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are thought to contribute to its medicinal properties. Infusions, teas, and dried extracts are among the most common herbal preparations made from Hieracium stenolonchum, although its use should be approached with caution due to potential allergenic and hepatotoxic effects.
